title: Functions That Do Real Work
objectives: Write functions with parameters, default values, and return statements
Use arrow functions and understand when they're appropriate
Build utility functions that solve practical problems
hints: A function should do one thing well — if you're writing 'and' in the description, consider splitting it
Arrow functions: const add = (a, b) => a + b; — great for short, simple operations
Default parameters: function greet(name = 'friend') lets you call greet() without arguments
assessmentCriteria: At least one function declaration and one arrow function are used
Functions use parameters with at least one default value
All functions return values (no relying on side effects alone)
Functions solve real calculation or formatting problems
